outputs.document.md:
# SDTM LB Domain Specification Document

## 1. Overview
This document specifies the structure and content of the SDTM LB (Laboratory Test Results) domain, containing 54,828 records. The LB domain contains data from laboratory tests performed on subjects during the course of a clinical trial.

## 2. LBTESTCD Mapping
The LBTESTCD variable represents the short name of the laboratory test or measurement. The following mapping conventions are applied:

### 2.1 Test Code Derivation
- LBTESTCD is derived from LBTEST using standard CDISC controlled terminology
- Test codes are 8-character maximum, uppercase, with no special characters except underscores
- Common test codes include:
  * ALB = Albumin
  * ALT = Alanine Aminotransferase
  * AST = Aspartate Aminotransferase
  * BILI = Bilirubin
  * CREAT = Creatinine
  * GLUC = Glucose
  * HGB = Hemoglobin
  * PLAT = Platelets
  * POT = Potassium
  * SOD = Sodium
  * WBC = White Blood Cells

### 2.2 Test Code Mapping Logic
- Local test names are mapped to standard LBTESTCD values using a predefined mapping table
- When no exact match exists, the most clinically appropriate code is selected
- New tests are mapped using the "OTH" (Other) code with the specific test name in LBTEST

## 3. Unit Conversion Logic
Unit standardization is applied to ensure consistent reporting across the study.

### 3.1 Standard Units
- Standard units are defined for each test based on CDISC recommendations
- Common standard units include:
  * g/L for Albumin
  * U/L for ALT, AST
  * µmol/L for Bilirubin
  * µmol/L for Creatinine
  * mmol/L for Glucose
  * g/L for Hemoglobin
  * 10^9/L for Platelets
  * mmol/L for Potassium, Sodium
  * 10^9/L for White Blood Cells

### 3.2 Conversion Process
1. Original units are captured in LBORRESU
2. Standard units are stored in LBSTRESU
3. Conversion factors are applied to LBORRES to calculate LBSTRES
4. Conversion logic is documented in the define.xml

### 3.3 Conversion Examples
- Creatinine: mg/dL to µmol/L (multiply by 88.4)
- Glucose: mg/dL to mmol/L (divide by 18)
- Hemoglobin: g/dL to g/L (multiply by 10)

## 4. Reference Range Handling
Reference ranges indicate the normal range of values for the laboratory test.

### 4.1 Reference Range Sources
- Reference ranges may be derived from:
  * Central laboratory ranges
  * Site-specific ranges
  * Demographic-specific ranges (age, sex)
  * Study-specific ranges

### 4.2 Reference Range Variables
- LBNRIND: Normal Range Indicator (Low, Normal, High)
- LBREFHI: Upper limit of normal range
- LBREFLO: Lower limit of normal range
- LBNRIND is derived based on LBSTRES, LBREFLO, and LBREFHI

### 4.3 Reference Range Logic
```
IF LBSTRES < LBREFLO THEN LBNRIND = "LOW"
IF LBSTRES > LBREFHI THEN LBNRIND = "HIGH"
IF LBSTRES >= LBREFLO AND LBSTRES <= LBREFHI THEN LBNRIND = "NORMAL"
```

### 4.4 Special Cases
- For tests where only one limit is applicable (e.g., tumor markers), only the relevant limit is populated
- When reference ranges change during the study, the appropriate range is applied based on LBBLFL (Baseline Flag) and visit timing

## 5. Specimen Type
The specimen type indicates the biological material tested.

### 5.1 Specimen Type Coding
- LBSPEC variable contains the specimen type
- Coded using CDISC controlled terminology
- Common specimen types include:
  * BLOOD = Whole blood
  * SERUM = Blood serum
  * PLASMA = Blood plasma
  * URINE = Urine
  * CSF = Cerebrospinal fluid

### 5.2 Specimen Type Derivation
- Specimen type is typically derived from the laboratory test name or collected separately
- When not explicitly provided, specimen type is inferred based on standard practices for the test

## 6. Supplemental Qualifier Mappings
Supplemental qualifiers provide additional context for laboratory results.

### 6.1 Timing Variables
- LBDTC: Date/Time of Collection
- LBDY: Study Day of Collection
- LBENDY: End Relative to Reference Period
- LBELTM: End Time of Collection

### 6.2 Method and Condition Variables
- LBMETHOD: Method used for the test
- LBFAST: Fasting status (Y, N, UNKNOWN)
- LBTOXGR: Toxicity Grade (if applicable)

### 6.3 Other Qualifiers
- LBCAT: Lab Category (e.g., "CHEMISTRY", "HEMATOLOGY", "URINALYSIS")
- LBPOS: Position of subject when specimen was collected
- LBREASND: Reason Not Done (if applicable)

## 7. Quality Control
- All LBTESTCD mappings are verified against CDISC controlled terminology
- Unit conversions are validated with test cases
- Reference ranges are reviewed for consistency across sites
- A 10% sample of records is verified for accuracy

## 8. Implementation Notes
- The LB domain contains 54,828 records
- All records have been mapped according to this specification
- Any deviations from this specification are documented in the define.xml
